revision.text: '''天棚機姫神'''(あめたなばたひめ)は[[日本神話]]に登場する女[[神]]である。'''天八千千比売命'''や'''天衣織女命'''という別名がある。あるいは天八千千比売命の母神である。また機織の神であることから[[栲幡千千姫命]]と同一神という説がある。 ==歴史== [[古語拾遺]]に、[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]に隠れた際、大神に献上する[[神衣和衣]]を織ったと書かれている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。 ==祀られている神社== *[[機殿神社]]([[三重県]][[松阪市]]) *[[本宮倭文神社]]([[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町) *[[倭文神社]]([[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町) *[[濱名惣社神明宮]]([[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[北区(浜松市)|北区]]) ==天棚機姫神が登場する作品== ===小説=== *浅葉なつ「[[神様の御用人]]」<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ 『神様の御用人』特集] - メディアワークス文庫</ref>

'''天棚機姫神'''(あめたなばたひめ)は[[日本神話]]に登場する女[[神]]である。'''天八千千比売命'''や'''天衣織女命'''という別名がある。あるいは天八千千比売命の母神である。また機織の神であることから[[栲幡千千姫命]]と同一神という説がある。 ==歴史== [[古語拾遺]]に、[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]に隠れた際、大神に献上する[[神衣和衣]]を織ったと書かれている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。 ==祀られている神社== *[[機殿神社]]([[三重県]][[松阪市]]) *[[本宮倭文神社]]([[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町) *[[倭文神社]]([[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町) *[[濱名惣社神明宮]]([[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[北区(浜松市)|北区]]) ==天棚機姫神が登場する作品== ===小説=== *浅葉なつ「[[神様の御用人]]」<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ 『神様の御用人』特集] - メディアワークス文庫</ref> ==脚注==

'''天棚機姫神'''(あめたなばたひめ)は[[日本神話]]に登場する女[[神]]である。'''天八千千比売命'''や'''天衣織女命'''という別名がある。あるいは天八千千比売命の母神である。また機織の神であることから[[栲幡千千姫命]]と同一神という説がある。 ==歴史== [[古語拾遺]]に、[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]に隠れた際、大神に献上する[[神衣和衣]]を織ったと書かれている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。 ==祀られている神社== *[[機殿神社]]([[三重県]][[松阪市]]) *[[本宮倭文神社]]([[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町) *[[倭文神社]]([[山梨県]][[韮崎市]]穂坂町) *[[濱名惣社神明宮]]([[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[北区 (浜松市)|北区]])

'''天棚機姫神'''(あめたなばたひめ)は[[日本神話]]に登場する女[[神]]である。'''天八千千比売命'''や'''天衣織女命'''という別名がある。あるいは天八千千比売命の母神である。また機織の神であることから[[栲幡千千姫命]]と同一神という説がある。 ==歴史== [[古語拾遺]]に、[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]に隠れた際、大神に献上する神衣和衣を織ったと書かれている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。

'''天棚機姫神'''(あめたなばたひめ)は[[日本神話]]に登場する女[[神]]である。'''天八千千比売命'''や'''天衣織女命'''という別名がある。あるいは天八千千比売命の母神である。また機織の神であることから[[栲幡千千姫命]]と同一神という説がある。 ==概要== [[古語拾遺]]に登場する。[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]から誘い出すために、神衣和衣を織ったとされている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。

==概要== [[古語拾遺]]に登場する。[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]から誘い出すために、神衣和衣を織ったとされている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。

'''天棚機姫神'''(あめたなばたひめ)は[[日本神話]]に登場する女[[神]]である。'''天八千千比売命'''や'''天衣織女命'''という別名がある。また[[機織]]の神であることから[[栲幡千千姫命]]と同一神という説がある。
==概要== [[古語拾遺]]に登場する。[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]から誘い出すために、神衣和衣を織ったとされている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。天棚機姫神は、天八千千比売命の母神であるという説があり、[[神道五部書]]の一つの倭姫命世記には、天八千千比売命は天棚機姫神の孫とされている。

{{デフォルトソート:あめたなばたひめ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]]

'''天棚機姫神'''<ref>[[古語拾遺]]</ref>(あめたなばたひめ)は[[日本神話]]に登場する女[[神]]である。'''天八千千比売命'''や'''天衣織女命'''という別名がある。また[[機織]]の神であることから[[栲幡千千姫命]]と同一神という説がある。
==概要== [[古語拾遺]]に登場する。[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]から誘い出すために、神衣和衣を織ったとされている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。天棚機姫神は、天八千千比売命の母神であるという説があり、[[神道五部書]]の一つの倭姫命世記には、天八千千比売命は天棚機姫神の孫とされている。また、茨城県神社誌では天速玉姫神の母神とされている。
== 祀られている神社 == * [[本宮倭文神社]]（[[山梨県]][[韮崎市]][[穂坂町]]（貴船神社。{{small|字}}[[穂坂町柳平|柳平]]）3195番地）<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 神社庁]</ref> * [[倭文神社 (韮崎市)|倭文神社]][[倭文神社降宮明神|降宮明神]]（降宮大明神、穂坂総社。山梨県韮崎市穂坂町{{small|字}}[[穂坂町宮久保|宮久保]]{{small|字}}[[宮久保降宮|降宮]]6051番地）<ref>各地の[[倭文神社]]も参照</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 神社庁]</ref>
* [[初生衣神社]]（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[北区_(浜松市)|北区]][[三ケ日町|三ヶ日町]]{{small|字}}[[三ヶ日町岡本|岡本]]698番地）<ref>[[遠江国]][[敷知郡]][[岡本村 (遠江国敷知郡)|岡本村]]、敷知郡（[[引佐郡]]）[[西浜名村]]{{small|字}}岡本</ref> * [[濱名惣社神明宮]]（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[北区_(浜松市)|北区]][[三ケ日町|三ヶ日町]]{{small|字}}[[三ヶ日町三ヶ日|三ヶ日]]122番地）<ref>[[遠江国]][[敷知郡]][[三ヶ日村]]、敷知郡（[[引佐郡]]）[[西浜名村]]{{small|字}}三ヶ日</ref>
* [[機殿神社]]（[[三重県]][[松阪市]][[大垣内町]] / [[井口中町]]） * [[松阪神社]]（三重県松阪市[[殿町 (松阪市)|殿町]]） * [[機物神社]]（[[大阪府]][[交野市]][[倉治]]）

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori). revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "Your Name"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the '''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}''':
* '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)}} (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|鈴鹿市]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)}} (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)}} (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)}} (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yurihama)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)}} (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)}} (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
Other notable Shidori Shrines include:
* '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)}} (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Oomiwa Shrine|ja|大甕神社}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.
== Related Topics ==
* '''{{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)

[[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]]

''Shidori Shrine'' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}'' (also known as ''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto'', ''Ameno Hachio'', or ''Takehatsuchi'') as the ''deity of weaving'', alongside ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}'' (also known as ''Amano Yachichihime'' or ''Amanoorihime''). These shrines were established by the ''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}'':
* ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * ''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)'' in ''Kuji District, Hitachi Province'' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Koma District, Kai Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Fuji [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]]

de ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天棚機姫神]]'' (also known as ''Amano Yachichihime'' or ''Amanoorihime''). These shrines were established by the ''{{ill|Shidori clan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:倭文氏]]'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:仁徳天皇]]'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:崇神天皇]]''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宮水神社]]'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''.

de ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天棚機姫神]]'' (also known as ''Amano Yachichihime'' or ''Amanoorihime''). These shrines were established by the ''{{ill|Shidori clan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:倭文氏]]'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:仁徳天皇]]'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:崇神天皇]]''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宮水神社]]'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:延喜式神名帳]]'':
* ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:伊勢崎市]], [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * ''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)'' in ''Kuji District, Hitachi Province'' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:静神社]] ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:那珂市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Koma District, Kai Province'' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|en}}[[Category:new {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:富士宮市]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine pages]][[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:舞鶴市]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Yosa District, Tango Province'' – {{ill|Shidori pages]][[ja:鳥取市]], [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * ''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)'' in ''Kawamura District, Hōki Province'' – {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] (Miyauchi, Additionally, ''{{ill|Ōmika Shrine|8=ja|9=大甕神社|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:大甕神社]]'' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:日立市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines ''Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto''. Similarly, ''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:石井神社]]'' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:笠間市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
'''Amatanabata-hime''' (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)

Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>

Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Kuraji)

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>

Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>

Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Kuraji)

Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Kuraji)

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Kuraji)

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Kuraji)
== Amatanabata-hime in Literature ==
=== Novels === * Natsu Asaba, **"{{ill|Kamisama no Goyōnin|ja|神様の御用人}}"**<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ ‘Kamisama no Goyōnin’ Feature] – MediaWorks Bunko</ref>

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Kuraji)

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Kuraji)
== Amatanabata-hime in Literature ==
=== Novels === * Natsu Asaba, **"{{ill|Kamisama no Goyōnin|ja|神様の御用人}}"**<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ ‘Kamisama no Goyōnin’ Feature] – MediaWorks Bunko</ref>

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Kuraji)
== Amatanabata-hime in Literature ==
=== Novels === * Natsu Asaba, **"{{ill|Kamisama no Goyōnin|ja|神様の御用人}}"**<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ ‘Kamisama no Goyōnin’ Feature] – MediaWorks Bunko</ref>

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=draft}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=draft}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|12=draft}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Katano, Osaka|ja|交野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Kuraji)

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

== Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) ==
Amatanabata-hime (天棚機姫神) is a **goddess** in **Japanese mythology**. She is also known as **Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto** (天八千千比売命) and **Amaorime-no-Mikoto** (天衣織女命). Due to her **association with weaving**, some believe she is the **same deity** as **Takahatachichihime-no-Mikoto** (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the **Kogo Shūi** (古語拾遺). She is said to have **woven sacred garments** to help lure **Amaterasu Ōmikami** (天照大御神) out of the **Heavenly Rock Cave** (天岩戸). Together with **Amanohazuchi-no-Kami** (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a **deity of weaving**.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the **mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto**. However, in the **Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki** (倭姫命世記), one of the **Five Books of Shinto** (神道五部書), she is referred to as the **grandmother** of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the **Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register**, she is said to be the **mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami** (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=draft}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mikkabi Town|ja|三ケ日町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamanaka Ward|ja|浜名区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|ja|浜松市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県|12=draft}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in **Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=draft}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in **Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).**</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nagoya, Aichi|ja|名古屋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Matsusaka, Mie|ja|松阪市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)

revision.text:
'''天棚機姫神'''<ref>[[古語拾遺]]</ref>(あめたなばたひめ)は[[日本神話]]に登場する女[[神]]である。'''天八千千比売命'''や'''天衣織女命'''という別名がある。また[[機織]]の神であることから[[栲幡千千姫命]]と同一神という説がある。
==概要== [[古語拾遺]]に登場する。[[天照大御神]]が[[天岩戸]]から誘い出すために、神衣和衣を織ったとされている。[[天羽槌雄神]]と共に機織の神として知られている。天棚機姫神は、天八千千比売命の母神であるという説があり、[[神道五部書]]の一つの倭姫命世記には、天八千千比売命は天棚機姫神の孫とされている。また、茨城県神社誌では天速玉姫神の母神とされている。
== 祀られている神社 == * [[倭文神社 (韮崎市)|本宮倭文神社]]（[[山梨県]][[韮崎市]][[穂坂町]]（貴船神社。{{small|字}}[[穂坂町柳平|柳平]]）3195番地）<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 神社庁]</ref> * [[倭文神社 (韮崎市)|倭文神社]]（降宮大明神、穂坂総社。山梨県韮崎市穂坂町{{small|字}}[[穂坂町宮久保|宮久保]]{{small|字}}[[宮久保降宮|降宮]]6051番地）<ref>各地の[[倭文神社]]も参照</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 神社庁]</ref>
* [[初生衣神社]]（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[浜名区]][[三ケ日町]]{{small|字}}[[三ヶ日町岡本|岡本]]698番地）<ref>[[遠江国]][[敷知郡]][[岡本村 (遠江国敷知郡)|岡本村]]、敷知郡（[[引佐郡]]）[[西浜名村]]{{small|字}}岡本</ref> * [[濱名惣社神明宮]]（[[静岡県]][[浜松市]][[浜名区]][[三ケ日町|三ヶ日町]]{{small|字}}[[三ヶ日町三ヶ日|三ヶ日]]122番地）<ref>[[遠江国]][[敷知郡]][[三ヶ日村]]、敷知郡（[[引佐郡]]）[[西浜名村]]{{small|字}}三ヶ日</ref> * [[多奈波太神社]]（[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[北区 (名古屋市)|北区]][[金城 (名古屋市)|金城]]四丁目13番16号） * [[機殿神社]]（[[三重県]][[松阪市]][[大垣内町]] / [[井口中町]]）
* [[松阪神社]]（三重県松阪市[[殿町 (松阪市)|殿町]]） * [[機物神社]]（[[大阪府]][[交野市]][[倉治]]）
==天棚機姫神が登場する作品== ===小説=== *浅葉なつ「[[神様の御用人]]」<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ 『神様の御用人』特集] - メディアワークス文庫</ref>
==出典== {{reflist}} {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}}
{{デフォルトソート:あめたなばたひめ}} [[category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:女神]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|qq=draft|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū Shitori Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|qq=|1=Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111674494}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|qq=|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuubuginu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ([[:en:Mikkabi, Shizuoka|Mikkabi Town]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaka Ward]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in '''Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|qq=|1=Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929616}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in '''Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatadono Shrine|qq=|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ([[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka Shrine|qq=|1=Matsusaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532402}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatamono Shrine|qq=|1=Hatamono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544512}} ([[:en:Katano|Katano, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Kuraji)
== Amatanabata-hime in Literature ==
=== Novels === * Natsu Asaba, '''"{{ill|Kamisama no Goyōnin|ja|神様の御用人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisama no Goyōnin|qq=|1=Kamisama no Goyōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688286}}"'''<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ ‘Kamisama no Goyōnin’ Feature] – MediaWorks Bunko</ref>

(also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
(also known as Amanoorihime)

<!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--> [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q48750893}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|qq=draft|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).

<!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--> [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q48750893}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|1=Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111674494}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ([[:en:Mikkabi, Shizuoka|Mikkabi Town]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaka Ward]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in '''Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|1=Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929616}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in '''Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatadono Shrine|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ([[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka Shrine|1=Matsusaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532402}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatamono Shrine|1=Hatamono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544512}} ([[:en:Katano|Katano, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Kuraji)

<!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--> [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q48750893}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
