<!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--> [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q48750893}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|1=Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111674494}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ([[:en:Mikkabi, Shizuoka|Mikkabi Town]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaka Ward]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in '''Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|1=Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929616}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in '''Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatadono Shrine|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ([[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka Shrine|1=Matsusaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532402}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatamono Shrine|1=Hatamono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544512}} ([[:en:Katano|Katano, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Kuraji)

<!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--><!--Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto--> [[Category:Japanese deities]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q48750893}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).

revision.comment: Bot: reorganize categories (alphabetical, deduplicated, moved to bottom)
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nirasaki, Yamanashi|Nirasaki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|1=Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111674494}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ([[:en:Mikkabi, Shizuoka|Mikkabi Town]], [[:en:Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|Hamanaka Ward]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu, Shizuoka]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]], Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in '''Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|1=Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929616}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in '''Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata Shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatadono Shrine|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ([[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka Shrine|1=Matsusaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532402}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatamono Shrine|1=Hatamono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544512}} ([[:en:Katano|Katano, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Kuraji)
== Amatanabata-hime in Literature ==
=== Novels === * Natsu Asaba, '''"{{ill|Kamisama no Goyōnin|ja|神様の御用人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisama no Goyōnin|1=Kamisama no Goyōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688286}}"'''<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ ‘Kamisama no Goyōnin’ Feature] – MediaWorks Bunko</ref>

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>

* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka Shrine|1=Matsusaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532402}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatamono Shrine|1=Hatamono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544512}} ([[:en:Katano|Katano, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Kuraji)

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|en|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|WD=Q290326|lt=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|1=Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111674494}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ({{ill|Mikkabi, Shizuoka|en|Mikkabi, Shizuoka|WD=Q11353987|lt=Mikkabi Town}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|WD=Q117077169|lt=Hamanaka Ward}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in '''Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|1=Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929616}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in '''Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatadono Shrine|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ({{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka Shrine|1=Matsusaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532402}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatamono Shrine|1=Hatamono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544512}} ({{ill|Katano|en|Katano|WD=Q819646|lt=Katano, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Kuraji)
== Amatanabata-hime in Literature ==
=== Novels === * Natsu Asaba, '''"{{ill|Kamisama no Goyōnin|ja|神様の御用人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisama no Goyōnin|1=Kamisama no Goyōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688286}}"'''<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ ‘Kamisama no Goyōnin’ Feature] – MediaWorks Bunko</ref>

(also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
(also known as Amanoorihime)

[[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48750893]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==

Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ({{ill|Mikkabi, Shizuoka|en|Mikkabi, Shizuoka|WD=Q11353987|lt=Mikkabi Town}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|WD=Q117077169|lt=Hamanaka Ward}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in '''Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|1=Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929616}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in '''Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatadono Shrine|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ({{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|en|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|WD=Q290326|lt=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|1=Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111674494}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ({{ill|Mikkabi, Shizuoka|en|Mikkabi, Shizuoka|WD=Q11353987|lt=Mikkabi Town}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|WD=Q117077169|lt=Hamanaka Ward}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in '''Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|1=Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929616}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in '''Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatadono Shrine|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ({{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka Shrine|1=Matsusaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532402}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatamono Shrine|1=Hatamono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544512}} ({{ill|Katano|en|Katano|WD=Q819646|lt=Katano, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Kuraji)

=== Novels === * Natsu Asaba, '''"{{ill|Kamisama no Goyōnin|ja|神様の御用人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisama no Goyōnin|1=Kamisama no Goyōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688286}}"'''<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ ‘Kamisama no Goyōnin’ Feature] – MediaWorks Bunko</ref>

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q48750893}} [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Goddesses]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto}}
[[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天棚機姫神]] {{Infobox deity}} '''Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto''' (天棚機姫神) is a '''goddess''' in '''Japanese mythology'''. She is also known as '''Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto''' (天八千千比売命) and '''Amaorime-no-Mikoto''' (天衣織女命). Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>
== Overview ==
Amatanabata-hime appears in the '''Kogo Shūi''' (古語拾遺). She is said to have '''woven sacred garments''' to help lure '''[[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]''' (天照大御神) out of the '''Heavenly Rock Cave''' (天岩戸). Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto. Additionally, according to the '''Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Register''', she is said to be the '''mother of Amahaya-Tamahime-no-Kami''' (天速玉姫神).
== Shrines Enshrining Amatanabata-hime ==
* {{ill|Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja|本宮倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongū Shitori Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|en|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|WD=Q290326|lt=Nirasaki, Yamanashi|1=Nirasaki, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Hosaka Town, Yanagidaira 3195)<ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6007 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref> * {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Nirasaki, Yamanashi)|1=Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111674494}} (降宮大明神, Hosaka Sōsha. Yamanashi Prefecture, Nirasaki City, Hosaka Town, Miyakubo 6051)<ref>See also various {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}.</ref><ref>[http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/6011 Yamanashi Shrine Association]</ref>
* {{ill|Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja|初生衣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsuubuginu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ({{ill|Mikkabi, Shizuoka|en|Mikkabi, Shizuoka|WD=Q11353987|lt=Mikkabi Town|1=Mikkabi, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|en|Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu|WD=Q117077169|lt=Hamanaka Ward|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu, Shizuoka|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, Okamoto 698)<ref>Formerly located in '''Okamoto Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|ja|濱名惣社神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamanasōsha Shinmei Shrine|1=Hamana Sōsha-shinmeigū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929616}} (Mikkabi Town, Hamanaka Ward, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Mikkabi 122)<ref>Formerly located in '''Mikkabi Village, Shikiji District (later Nishihama Village, Inasa District, Shizuoka Province).'''</ref> * {{ill|Tanabata Shrine|ja|多奈波太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabata Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya, Aichi|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Kita Ward, Kinjō 4-13-16) * {{ill|Hatadono Shrine|ja|機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatadono Shrine|1=Hatadono Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544511}} ({{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka, Mie|1=Matsusaka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Ōgaito Town / Iguchi Naka Town)
* {{ill|Matsusaka Shrine|ja|松阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsusaka Shrine|1=Matsusaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11532402}} (Matsusaka City, Mie Prefecture, Tonochō) * {{ill|Hatamono Shrine|ja|機物神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatamono Shrine|1=Hatamono Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544512}} ({{ill|Katano|en|Katano|WD=Q819646|lt=Katano, Osaka|1=Katano}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Kuraji)
== Amatanabata-hime in Literature ==
=== Novels === * Natsu Asaba, '''"{{ill|Kamisama no Goyōnin|ja|神様の御用人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisama no Goyōnin|1=Q28688286|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688286}}"'''<ref>[http://mwbunko.com/special/sp06/ ‘Kamisama no Goyōnin’ Feature] – MediaWorks Bunko</ref>

(also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}

Due to her '''association with weaving''', some believe she is the '''same deity''' as '''[[Takuhadachiji-hime]]''' (栲幡千千姫命).<ref>[[Kogo Shūi]]</ref>

Together with {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt='''Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神), she is widely revered as a '''deity of weaving'''.
Some traditions describe Amatanabata-hime as the '''mother of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto'''. However, in the '''Yamatohime-no-Mikoto Seiki''' (倭姫命世記), one of the '''Five Books of Shinto''' (神道五部書), she is referred to as the '''grandmother''' of Amahachichihime-no-Mikoto.
